data.title: PepsiCo acquires $585 million in Celsius stock, increasing stake to 11%, boosting company's energy and stock market value.
data.description: In a recent agreement, PepsiCo purchased $585 million of newly issued convertible stock, increasing its ownership in Celsius to 11%. This deal has given Celsius a significant boost, as detailed in a recent report from Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Mobvista's 2025 interim results show $938 million revenue, 47% increase, $88.7 million EBITDA, 41% jump.
data.description: Mobvista, a prominent technology platform catering to global mobile developers, has unveiled its interim outcomes for the six months concluding on June 30, 2025. The company evidenced substantial development and profitability during this period, with its primary programmatic ad platform, Mintegral, being a key contributor to its success. Mobvista disclosed a revenue of $938 million, displaying a noteworthy 47% surge compared to the previous year. Additionally, the adjusted EBITDA stood at $88.7 million, marking a substantial 41% increase from the prior period.

data.title: Economists predict inflation to stay above central bank's 2% target through 2021.
data.description: According to a group of economists, inflation is anticipated to stay significantly higher than the central bank's 2 percent target for the remainder of the year.

data.title: Stocks drop, Gold surges, Silver at 24-year highs due to rising inflation. Core PCE index rose 2.9% yoy in July, affecting investors and markets negatively. Nasdaq 100 fell 1.3%, Nvidia Corp declined 3.4% by midday in NY. Tech and high-growth stocks underperformed. Market uncertainty over potential interest rate cuts. Fed Chair Powell previously hinted at rate reductions.
data.description: Investor sentiment turned sour on Friday as a key inflation metric closely monitored by the Federal Reserve surged to a five-month high, sparking uncertainty about the timing of potential interest rate cuts. The core Personal Consumption Expenditures index, the Fed's preferred inflation measure, climbed 2.9% year-over-year in July, meeting expectations but hitting a five-month peak. This data rattled markets after Fed Chair Jerome Powell hinted at possible rate reductions. By midday in New York, major stock indices were down, particularly in the technology and high-growth sectors. Riskier assets like cryptocurrencies also saw steep declines. The Nasdaq 100 dropped 1.3%, ending a three-day rally. Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ: NVDA) took a significant 3.4% hit, contrasting with a minor 0.8% decrease the day before.

data.title: Asante Gold Corp. receives $237M from equity offering, completes Kinross restructuring, satisfying escrow release conditions.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on August 12, 2025, Asante Gold Corporation (CSE: ASE | GSE: ASG | FRANKFURT:1A9 | OTCQX: ASGOF) announced the successful execution of definitive agreements for a Financing Package worth around $500 million. This achievement allowed the company to meet the conditions for the first drawdown under the agreements, leading to the release of escrow conditions tied to a bought deal private placement. The placement involved 163,300,000 subscription receipts priced at C$1.45 each, generating total gross proceeds of approximately C$237 million. The Offering was completed on July 7, 2025, with all figures reported in U.S. dollars unless specified otherwise.

data.title: LayerZero (ZRO) struggles at $1.98 after gaining from Stargate deal. Price falls by 1.59%, support at $1.85. RSI: 48.30.
data.description: The price of ZRO has decreased to $1.98, marking a 1.59% decline as post-acquisition momentum diminishes. Technical analysis indicates a neutral RSI of 48.30, and identifies crucial support at $1.85.

data.title: NewtekOne, Inc. closed an offering of 2,000,000 depositary shares generating $48.357 million in net proceeds. Series B Preferred Stock will pay a 8.5% per annum cash dividend.
data.description: On August 20, 2025, NewtekOne, Inc. (NASDAQ: NEWT) closed an offering of 2,000,000 depositary shares, each representing a 1/40th interest in their 8.500% Fixed-Rate Reset Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series B, with a liquidation preference of $1,000 per share. This raised around $48.357 million for the company. Additionally, the underwriters were granted a 30-day option to buy up to an additional 150,000 Depositary Shares at the public offering price less the underwriting discount. The Series B Preferred Stock will pay a non-cumulative 8.500% per annum cash dividend starting from October 1, 2025, until October 1, 2030, with future dividend rates based on the five-year US treasury rate and a fixed spread.

data.title: SentinelOne's Q2 revenue hits $242.2M, up 22% y-o-y, ARR at $1B, large customers spending >$100,000 annually up 23%.
data.description: SentinelOne Inc. (NYSE: S) exceeded expectations in their second-quarter fiscal 2026 results, with revenue reaching $242.2 million, up 22% year-over-year. Non-GAAP EPS was 4 cents, beating estimates of 3 cents. Annual recurring revenue (ARR) grew by 24% to $1 billion, with large customers spending over $100,000 annually increasing by 23% to 1,513. They closed the quarter with $1.2 billion in cash and investments. Management projected third-quarter revenue of $256 million and raised full-year revenue expectations to $998 million-$1.002 billion. Wall Street analysts reiterated bullish views, with WestPark Capital analyst Casey Ryan reiterating a Buy rating and a $25 price forecast, praising SentinelOne's growth and profitability. SentinelOne's gross margins increased to 73.3%, aligning with their progress and analyst projections.

data.title: Fed's Jackson Hole Symposium begins, focus on Powell's speech on rate cuts. VOO ETF down 2%. Topic: "Labor Markets in Transition." Fed eyes inflation, tariffs. Powell to address in Sept. 17 meeting decision. Upside risk to inflation greater than unemployment, tariffs impacting goods price inflation. Hard to quantify tariff impact on inflation.
data.description: The Federal Reserve's Jackson Hole Economic Symposium is set to begin with Chair Jerome Powell's speech on Friday at 10 a.m. ET. Markets are eager for hints on interest rate cuts, though Powell is unlikely to make any commitments yet. The VOO ETF has dropped nearly 2% this week. The theme of the event is "Labor Markets in Transition," reflecting the Fed's struggle with a cooling job market and inflation above target. Powell will discuss the "Economic Outlook and Framework Review," offering clues on the Fed's outlook ahead of its September 17 rate decision. The majority of officials, as per the Fed's July meeting minutes, see upside risk to inflation as greater than unemployment, noting tariff effects on data and goods price inflation. The impact of tariffs on inflation is hard to quantify, but many see domestic businesses affected. (Characters: 857)

data.title: Frontline Road Safety acquires Peek Pavement Marking in Georgia, expanding its services in Southeast U.S. Founded in 1962, Peek offers road safety services and marked growth for the company. Transaction amount undisclosed.
data.description: Frontline Road Safety, the top U.S. pavement marking services provider, has recently acquired Peek Pavement Marking, a renowned company specializing in pavement marking and road safety services in Columbus, Georgia. Peek was established in 1962 by Donnie Peek and later joined by Louis McCosh in 1967, originally operating as a small business focusing on painting parking lots. The specific financial details of this private acquisition were not disclosed to the public.

data.title: Kimberly-Clark pays $40.4M for fraudulently selling adulterated surgical gowns, violating AAMI Level 4 standards.
data.description: Kimberly-Clark Corporation has agreed to pay $40.4 million in a settlement for a criminal charge involving the sale of adulterated MicroCool surgical gowns. The Justice Department revealed that the company confessed to deceptive testing and mislabeling the gowns as meeting the top AAMI Level 4 protection standards. A criminal case in the Northern District of Texas alleges that Kimberly-Clark knowingly introduced substandard medical devices into interstate commerce with the aim to deceive. data.title: Two-day seminar on U.S. power industry, renewable energy, ISO markets, and power transactions in Charlotte, NC on Oct 7-8, 2025.
data.description: A training course titled "Today's U.S. Electric Power Industry, Renewable Energy, ISO Markets, and Power Transactions" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's portfolio. The course will take place in Charlotte, NC, United States on October 7-8, 2025. It aims to enhance understanding of the U.S. electric power sector and the various mechanisms involved in power transactions. The seminar offers a detailed overview of the industry's structure, operations, and current trends. This comprehensive program is ideal for individuals seeking insights into the U.S. electric power industry landscape.

data.title: KOIL Energy Solutions awarded a significant contract for subsea isolation valve control equipment to protect offshore platforms.
data.description: On August 29, 2025, Koil Energy Solutions, Inc. (OTCQB: KLNG), a global expert in subsea technology, revealed the acquisition of a noteworthy contract. This deal involves the provision of control equipment for a subsea isolation valve system designed to safeguard offshore platforms and workers from the accidental discharge of hydrocarbons from subsea wells. The contract involves the full scope of engineering, procurement, and production of KOIL's reliable and established technical system for Subsea Safety Control Systems. This development marks a significant step for Koil Energy Solutions, Inc. in the specialized sector of subsea equipment and services.

data.title: OM price plunges 6.6% to $0.22 amid bearish pressure, eyes $0.21 support. Technicals signal further downside.
data.description: The price of OM token has fallen by 6.59% to $0.22, as per recent trading data. Technical indicators suggest bearish momentum for the token as it nears crucial support levels.

data.title: Refresco acquires Norway's Telemark Kildevann, a top beverage maker, in latest expansion move.
data.description: Press Release: Refresco has finalized the acquisition of Telemark Kildevann, a prominent beverage manufacturer based in Norway. The deal involves a substantial exchange of assets, estimated to be worth several million dollars. This acquisition is set to significantly expand Refresco's presence in the Norwegian market and strengthen its position as a key player in the beverage industry.
